plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the full plan for optimizing `src/pipeline.ts`. Every function gets a targeted, surgical fix — only that one file needs to change:

---

### What will be done

| Function | Current | Fix | Complexity change |
|---|---|---|---|
| `groupByCategory` | `array.find()` per transaction + `reduce()` recalculation | `Map<category, group>`, O(1) lookup, incremental `total +=` | O(n²) → O(n) |
| `findDuplicates` | Nested i/j loop + inner linear group scan | Single pass `Map<key, group>`, filter groups with ≥ 2 entries | O(n²) → O(n) |
| `calculateRunningBalance` | Re-filters + re-sorts + re-sums from scratch on every position | Filter once, sort once, single accumulating pass | O(n·m²) → O(n + m log m) |
| `topSpenders` | `includes()` dedup + per-user full scan + bubble sort | `Map<userId, {total,count}>` accumulator + native `.sort()` | O(n²) → O(n + u log u) |
| `searchTransactions` | New `RegExp` per transaction + impossible-dedup scan | Hoist `RegExp` before loop, use `.filter()` | O(n) overhead → clean O(n) |
| `processAll` | — | No change needed | — |

### Correctness preserved
- `findDuplicates` key format stays `userId|amount|date` (matches the test's `"alice|50|2025-01-15"` assertion)
- `calculateRunningBalance` keeps the same `Math.round(balance * 100) / 100` rounding
- `topSpenders` keeps `Math.round` and sorts descending
- `searchTransactions` keeps the same regex escape logic, just moved outside the loop
